An approximate Skater algorithm implemented using igraph methods.

Source: R/fast_skater.R
skater_igraph.Rd

Leverages C implementations in igraph package. The approach collects all edges from all clusters, sorts by global cost, and iterates until a valid split is found.

Usage

skater_igraph(
  edges,
  data,
  ncuts,
  crit,
  vec.crit,
  method = "euclidean",
  p = 2,
  cov = NULL,
  inverted = FALSE
)

Arguments

edges

igraph edges. Graph edges of the minimum spanning tree object as returned by igraph::as_edgelist().

data

dataframe (columns of numeric values). Dataframe columns containing values for each graph node of the desired variables (referred to as additional_variable_cols elsewhere).

ncuts

integer. The number of cuts to make in the minimum spanning tree to generate the desired number of clusters.

crit

integer. The desired number of nodes required to form a cluster. Should align with ncuts to approximate the total number of data points in the input data.

vec.crit

numeric vector. The minimum and maximum crit values. If this argument is missing it defaults to c(crit, Inf), meaning no maximum cluster size.

method

character. Distance calculation method passed onto ssw() for computing edge costs. Euclidean, manhattan and mahalanobis are internally defined options, other options are passed onto dist().

p

numeric. Power of the Minkowski distance, as in spdep::ssw(). Default = 2.

cov

numeric matrix. Covariance matrix used to compute mahalanobis distance in ssw. Only required if method="mahalanobis". Default = NULL.

inverted

Bool. If 'TRUE' cov is supposed to contain the inverse of the covariance matrix.

On this page

Developed by Vanessa Haller-Bull, Benjamin Grier, Takuya Iwanaga.
